key_insight: The paper shows that a purpose-built automated seam welding system can deliver more consistent pipe joining by combining rigid fixturing, controlled rotation, and pneumatic clamping.
engineering_relevance: It is relevant to manufacturing automation because it reduces manual variability, improves operator safety, and enables more uniform weld quality in cylindrical pipe production.
method: Designed and fabricated an automatic seam welding rig with a mechanical frame, workpiece fixtures, and programmable pneumatic clamps.
Used a gear-motor drive to maintain controlled pipe rotation at 10–15 RPM for continuous weld-bead formation.
Compared welding performance on CRCS and ER34 pipes while evaluating current and wall-thickness effects; added internal water-cooled CuCrZr rotating wheel electrodes for heat control.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a leakage-failure reduction from 15% in the baseline setup to below 3% after implementing the automated system and cooling strategy.
watch_item: Performance appears validated on a limited material and parameter range, so scale-up to other pipe sizes, alloys, or higher-throughput conditions may require re-tuning.
paper_title: Design and Development of Automatic Seam Welding of Pipes
